Round 2 goals: (1) research OSS that stores app data in git (git-bug=CRDTs-in-git, Radicle COBs, git-appraise, public-inbox=mailing-lists-in-git, git-ssb, Dolt, Fossil) to JUSTIFY why-git; (2) add a strong 'Why Git' section; (3) user warns 'truly useful, not just complicated' → be willing to CUT/tier the v1.1 machinery, lead with minimal useful core; (4) get codex feedback this time.

Prioritize i5h as a minimal Git-native coordination receipt protocol: adopt CloudEvents-style distinct event identity, Radicle/git-bug-style append-only Git operations, A2A/ACP-style derived task lifecycle and small advertised capabilities, plus advisory claim leases to reduce duplicate coding-agent work; defer transport-scale mechanisms until measurements justify them.
RISK: useful offline claims cannot be exclusive during partitions. Any CLAIM/lease extension must surface concurrent claims after merge and remain advisory rather than promise locking.

Research round 2: (A) Git-as-DB OSS strongly justifies git substrate — git-bug (op-based CRDT in git objects, Lamport clocks over commit DAG, rejects wall-clock), Radicle COBs (CRDTs in git, SIGNS THE REFS so peers verify without trusting node, Ed25519), git-appraise (refs/notes one-JSON-per-line, cat_sort_uniq merge = grow-only-set CRDT — EXACTLY h5i's union-merge), public-inbox (one-blob-per-message append log, pull-based subscription, 'no database'). h5i already mirrors public-inbox+git-appraise. Limits: don't use one-ref-per-message; git isn't a query engine (build index); supply your own semantics. (B) Minimalism: Worse-is-Better (impl simplicity → adoption), RFC 6709 ('few options→ubiquity', unknown fields MUST be ignored, don't pre-extend), Postel critique (liberal accept of loose optional fields → decay), FIPA-ACL FAILED due to 20+ performatives + mandatory ontology — direct warning for our kind taxonomy. Checklist: core feature only if universal+strict+afternoon-implementable+no-shared-dep+interop-without-it+human-readable+concrete-use-now.

Fix: watch now peek(advance=false)+in-memory dedup (never touches persistent cursor); added msg::mark_seen(); Stop hook + deliver_codex_inbox now deliver-then-ack (peek→emit→mark_seen). (2) Identity flip: resolve_identity chain is override > H5I_AGENT env > stored .git/.h5i/msg/identity > literal 'claude'. This is a SHARED clone (cursors/claude.json + cursors/codex.json). The stored identity file holds 'codex'. So any claude command lacking the env var (e.g. hooks/background tasks not carrying harness injection) silently falls back to stored 'codex' and impersonates codex. CORRECTED root cause. Env precedence WORKS: controlled --plain test shows H5I_AGENT=claude -> agent claude; H5I_AGENT=zztest -> agent zztest; env unset -> agent codex (stored fallback). REAL bug: when H5I_AGENT is absent, resolve_identity silently falls back to the SHARED .git/.h5i/msg/identity file (=codex).
